expressions.english: street; ground
shortcut
uphill road
dead end
expressions.notes: 막다르다 = to be a dead end

expressions.english: to lose one's way; to get lost
to walk the road
to cross the road
to ask the way

I think I lost the way.
I think I am lost.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ 것 같다 = it seems/looks like + S + V; I think + S + V

expressions.english:
May I ask you how to get there?
Do you mind telling me the way to get there?
I don't think this is the way.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
아니다 = to be not

expressions.english: If you go this way, it is faster.
I know a shortcut.
Which way should I go from here?

expressions.korean: 어떤 사람이 길을 막고 서 있어요.
expressions.english:
Someone is blocking the road.
There is someone standing in the road, blocking it.
expressions.notes:
어떤 사람 = someone; which person
막다 = to block
서 있다 = to be standing

expressions.korean: 길을 물어보는 게 좋을 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think it's better if we ask for directions.
Please tell me which way is the way out.
expressions.notes:
나가다 = to go out
알려 주다 = to let someone know; to teach something to someone

expressions.korean: 이 길이 아닌가 봐요. 여긴 막다른 길이에요.
expressions.english: This must not be the way. It is a dead end.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄴ가 봐다 = I think/guess + S + be + N, N + must be + adjective
여긴 is a contraction of 여기 + 는.

expressions.korean: 어제 길을 걸어다가 친구를 우연히 만났어요.
expressions.english: I met a friend by chance yesterday while walking down the street.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
우연히 = by chance
만나다 = to meet (up)

expressions.korean: 길을 건널 때는 차가 오나 안 오나 잘 보고 건너야 돼요.
expressions.english: When crossing the street, you must carefully look to see if a car is coming or not, then proceed.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
차 = car
-나 안 -나 = if + S + V or not
잘 = well; carefully
